a quatrain about making use of favorable winds in sailing your ship yet being prepared for  windless days

With sails unfurled, we ride the gale,
Seizing winds that drive us far.
Our ship, she leaps on waves like whales,
This boundless sea our reservoir.

Yet ebb and flow sometimes decease,
Leaving us in stillness, at ease.
Steadfast, prepared we shall remain,
For listless seas return to life, unchained.
one eloquent  quatrain about making use of favorable winds in sailing your ship yet being prepared for windless days

On gusts of fortune, we set sail,
Embracing favoring winds that blow,
Yet mindful of the calm's travail,
In windless voids, our strength must grow.
one eloquent quatrain about making use of favorable winds in sailing your ship yet being prepared for windless days

With eager hearts, we catch the breeze,
Our vessel soars o'er crested seas,
But in the lulls, our skills refined,
For windless days, poised and primed.

For quiet realms demand our spade.
